logs: Constructed 5 slice handles: A, B, C, D, E
summary: Full RLM trace over a corpus FILE HANDLE: decompose into per-slice handles, recurse one subagent per slice, validate separately, aggregate in workflow JS

phases.title: Decompose
phases.detail: slicer reads the corpus handle and writes one file per @@SLICE block + a validator handle

phases.title: Recurse
phases.detail: one subagent per constructed slice handle; each reads only its slice and returns a structured record

phases.title: Validate
phases.detail: a separate subagent reads only the @@VALIDATOR handle and returns the token

phases.title: Aggregate
phases.detail: workflow JS filters active records, sorts by rank, joins words with hyphens, sums weights

workflowProgress.label: decompose:slicer
workflowProgress.promptPreview:
You are the ROOT step of a Recursive Language Model (RLM).
You are given a CORPUS FILE HANDLE (a path). Treat it as opaque context state.
CORPUS HANDLE: /home/raw/github-rawwerks/aiewf-2026-rlm-recursive-coding-agent-talk/experiments/claude-workflows-full-rlm/corpus.md
SLICE OUTPUT DIRECTORY: /home/raw/github-rawwerks/aiewf-2026-rlm-recursive-coding-agent-talk/experiments/claude-workflows-full-rlm…

workflowProgress.label: validator
workflowProgress.promptPreview:
You are a dedicated VALIDATOR sub-call.
Read ONLY this file: /home/raw/github-rawwerks/aiewf-2026-rlm-recursive-coding-agent-talk/experiments/claude-workflows-full-rlm/generated-slices/validator.md
It contains a line "validator: <TOKEN>". Return JSON: { validator: <TOKEN> } with the exact token, no extra text.
